industries.name: Cemented Carbide Cutting Tools
uses: Tungsten carbide (WC) bonded with cobalt metal binder (WC-Co, cemented carbide): the dominant cutting tool material for metal machining — WC's hardness (9.5 Mohs, second only to diamond) and cobalt's toughness combine to give cutting tool inserts 5–10× longer life than high-speed steel

WC-Co inserts (turning, milling, drilling, boring) for machining steel, cast iron, aluminum, titanium, and nickel superalloys in automotive, aerospace, and general engineering. Billions of indexable inserts consumed annually worldwide
Solid carbide end mills and drills for precision machining of hardened steels, composites, and exotic alloys in aerospace, die/mold, and medical device manufacturing
Mining and tunneling tools: WC-Co drill bits, road milling picks, and tunnel boring machine cutter discs — tungsten carbide is the only economically viable material for rock drilling
WC-Co wear parts: nozzles, dies, drawing dies, seal rings, and valve components in oil & gas, mining, and industrial processing where abrasion resistance is paramount

companies.name: Sandvik Coromant
companies.use: World's largest cutting tool manufacturer. Produces WC-Co inserts, solid carbide end mills, and drilling tools for automotive, aerospace, and general engineering. Major tungsten carbide consumer from powder to finished tools.

companies.name: Kennametal
companies.use: Major cemented carbide cutting tool and wear part manufacturer. Produces WC-Co inserts, mining bits, and wear-resistant components. Also produces powdered tungsten carbide for other manufacturers.

companies.name: Iscar (IMC Group, Berkshire Hathaway)
companies.use: Major cutting tool manufacturer producing WC-Co inserts and tooling systems for metal cutting. Part of Berkshire Hathaway's IMC Group with Iscar, Ingersoll, and other cutting tool brands globally.

companies.name: China Minmetals / CNMC
companies.use: China dominates world tungsten production — primary from scheelite and wolframite mines in Jiangxi, Hunan, and Fujian provinces. China controls ~65% of world reserves and ~80% of production. Major APT (ammonium paratungstate) exporter.

industries.name: Steel Alloying & High-Speed Steel
uses: High-speed steel (HSS): M2 (6%W-5%Mo-4%Cr-2%V), M42 (cobalt HSS), and T-series (18%W) steels for cutting tools that maintain hardness at red heat — tungsten forms stable W₂C and other carbides that resist softening at 500–600°C during high-speed machining
Hot work tool steels (H21, H23) containing 9–18% tungsten for brass and copper extrusion dies, forging dies, and die-casting tooling — tungsten maintains hardness at elevated working temperatures

Tungsten heavy alloys (W-Ni-Fe or W-Ni-Cu, 90–97% W): extremely high density (17–18.5 g/cm³) used in radiation shielding, gyroscope rotors, aircraft counterweights, armor-piercing kinetic energy penetrators (military), and dart barrels
Stellite (Co-Cr-W) hardfacing alloys for wear-resistant valve seats, saw teeth, and agricultural tillage points — tungsten increases hardness at elevated temperatures

Tungsten filaments for incandescent and halogen lamps: tungsten's melting point (3422°C, the highest of all metals) allows operation at 2400–3000K — now a declining use as LED lighting displaces filament lamps

companies.name: Plansee Group
companies.use: Major tungsten metal products manufacturer. Produces tungsten rod, sheet, wire, and powder for lighting filaments, high-temperature furnace parts, sputtering targets, and electrical contacts. Significant tungsten heavy alloy producer.

companies.name: Buffalo Tungsten (Tungsten Heavy Powder)
companies.share: ~10% of North American tungsten powder market
companies.use: Major US tungsten powder and heavy alloy producer. Produces tungsten metal powder, WC powder, and tungsten heavy alloys for radiation shielding, aerospace counterweights, and defense kinetic energy penetrators.

industries.name: Electronics & Lighting
uses: Tungsten metallization in semiconductor chips: tungsten CVD plug fill for contact and via holes in silicon, and tungsten word line gates in 3D NAND flash memory — tungsten's high melting point allows use as refractory metal interconnect in high-temperature processing steps
Tungsten sputtering targets for PVD deposition of W diffusion barrier layers, TiW adhesion layers, and W word lines in DRAM and NAND memory manufacturing

X-ray tube anodes: tungsten (or tungsten-rhenium) rotating anodes in medical and industrial X-ray tubes — tungsten's high melting point, high atomic number (Z=74, efficient X-ray production), and thermal conductivity make it the only viable anode material

TIG (tungsten inert gas) welding electrodes: pure W, W-ThO₂, W-CeO₂, or W-LaO₂ electrodes for GTAW welding of aluminum, titanium, stainless steel, and specialty alloys — tungsten's melting point allows it to sustain the welding arc without melting

companies.name: Applied Materials
companies.use: Major semiconductor equipment company whose CVD systems deposit tungsten plugs and tungsten word lines in DRAM and NAND memory manufacturing. Major indirect consumer of tungsten hexafluoride (WF₆) CVD precursor.

companies.name: Varex Imaging (Varian spinoff)
companies.use: World's largest X-ray tube manufacturer. Produces tungsten and tungsten-rhenium rotating anode discs for medical CT, radiography, and mammography X-ray tubes, and tungsten targets for industrial and security inspection systems.
